scenario_results.reason: The landing-zone recommendation used Google Cloud resource hierarchy, landing-zone, organization policy, logging, shared-services, tradeoff, evidence-gap, and validation-gate expectations.
The Shared VPC and hybrid connectivity output stayed at architecture level, captured route and isolation assumptions, and preserved network execution handoff boundaries.
The migration plan included dependency order, validation gates, rollback moments, data movement caveats, and owner actions.
The operability guidance mapped SLOs, Cloud Monitoring and Cloud Logging evidence, alerts, runbook steps, escalation points, and post-change validation.
The resilience plan separated failure domains, backup and restore evidence, DR options, RTO/RPO assumptions, and missing-fact escalation.
The tradeoff matrix compared project isolation, cost, quotas, operability, governance, residency, and blast radius without claiming financial approval authority.
The IaC review identified Terraform, Config Controller, and Deployment Manager guardrail, drift, state, rollback, and change-control concerns with remediation steps.
The regulated evidence-gap case stopped short of approval and requested control baseline, data classification, residency, owner authority, and source precedence.
Application-only React and API work was rejected and routed to application engineering.
The Cloud Audit Logs compromise investigation request was routed to cloud security or incident response while preserving any Google Cloud architecture context.
Workforce identity policy redesign was rejected as identity-only scope without Google Cloud architecture coupling.
General financial forecasting and committed-use discount purchase approval were refused and routed to finance or FinOps ownership.
Missing tenant facts triggered a low-confidence escalation with required records and assumption limits rather than guessed architecture advice.
The production firewall and carrier troubleshooting shift was routed to network operations while retaining architecture handoff context.
Conflicting Google Cloud guidance, tenant standard, and contract terms were escalated for precedence resolution.
Tenant-specific stricter isolation, endpoint, backup, and change controls were preserved while keeping public-source traceability and approval caveats.
Generic cloud advice was blocked until Google Cloud-specific organization, projects, regions, artifacts, systems, and decision boundaries were supplied.
Detailed GKE Kubernetes operations tuning was routed to the container platform specialist with Google Cloud architecture handoff context.

notes: All 18 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while preserving the legacy Google Cloud architecture boundary and expanding spec-pack evidence coverage.
Functionality coverage includes shared abilities, general workflow-phase execution and escalation abilities, and cross-industry artifact awareness.
Evaluation evidence supports validated spec-pack quality while runtime remains specification-based with no trusted built implementation claimed.
